component.name: FullScreenSheet
component.description: Full-viewport overlay with a scroll-condensing header, body, and footer for immersive detail views.

useCases: Detail views that need the full viewport (e.g., record detail, document viewer)
Multi-step flows or wizards that overlay the main UI
Immersive editing experiences with header metadata that condenses on scroll
Full-screen previews with sticky footer actions

requiredProps.name: open
requiredProps.description: Controls whether the sheet is visible

requiredProps.name: onClose
requiredProps.description: Called when the user presses Escape or the close button

commonPatterns.name: detail-view
commonPatterns.description: Full-screen detail view with title, description, body content, and footer actions
commonPatterns.composition:
Record Detail ID: 12345 | Created: Jan 1
Content here

commonPatterns.name: with-header-actions
commonPatterns.description: Header with action buttons alongside the title

antiPatterns.scenario: Nesting a FullScreenSheet inside another FullScreenSheet
antiPatterns.reason: Both lock body scroll and occupy the full viewport, causing z-index and scroll conflicts
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a standard Sheet or AlertDialog for nested overlays

antiPatterns.scenario: Forgetting to pass onClose to both FullScreenSheet and FullScreenSheetHeader
antiPatterns.reason: The root handles Escape key; the header renders the close button — both need the handler
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass onClose to both components

antiPatterns.scenario: Placing content directly in FullScreenSheet without FullScreenSheetBody
antiPatterns.reason: The scroll-condensing header depends on the body ref to detect scroll position
antiPatterns.alternative: Always use FullScreenSheetBody for the scrollable content area

headerState.description: Header auto-condenses when body scrolls past 10px: padding shrinks, title/description collapse to a single row, and a shadow appears

slots.header: FullScreenSheetHeader with close button, title, description, and optional actions
slots.title: FullScreenSheetTitle renders an h2 that shrinks in condensed state
slots.description: FullScreenSheetDescription renders metadata that truncates in condensed state
slots.actions: FullScreenSheetActions for buttons placed in the header row
slots.body: FullScreenSheetBody is the scrollable content area that drives header condensing
slots.footer: FullScreenSheetFooter with sticky bottom bar for primary actions

interactions.open: Renders a fixed full-viewport overlay with fade-in animation
interactions.close: Escape key or close button triggers onClose
interactions.scroll: Scrolling the body past 10px condenses the header: padding decreases, title font shrinks, description truncates, shadow appears
interactions.bodyLock: document.body overflow is set to hidden while open and restored on close

responsive.mobile: Full viewport; header and footer respect the max-w-7xl container
responsive.desktop: Full viewport; content area centered with max-w-7xl

accessibility.role: Generic div with fixed positioning; functions as a modal overlay
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Escape to close; Tab navigates through focusable elements within the sheet
accessibility.screenReader: Close button has aria-label 'Close'; title renders as h2 for heading hierarchy
accessibility.focusManagement: No explicit focus trap — consider adding one for strict modal compliance

rules: The sheet renders nothing when open is false
Body scroll is locked (overflow: hidden) while the sheet is open
Header condensing is driven by a scroll listener on the body element (threshold: 10px)
In condensed state the header switches from column layout to row layout
FullScreenSheetFooter is sticky at the bottom with a top border
The body ref is shared via context — FullScreenSheetBody must be used for scroll detection to work
Max content width is capped at max-w-7xl in header and footer

aiHints.context: Use for immersive full-viewport views like record details or multi-step flows. The scroll-condensing header is the key feature — it shrinks the title and description as the user scrolls. Always use the full sub-component hierarchy: FullScreenSheet > Header + Body + Footer. Pass onClose to both the root and the header.
